channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: My Mastra Agent Found a Production Bug in Five Minutes
item.description: I saw a tweet about Sentry shipping an AI agent that watches your error logs and auto-suggests fixes. I thought: I can build that. So I did — from my phone, on a Friday afternoon, while walking...

item.title: The Production Agent Gap: Why Your AI Agent Tutorial Won't Survive Real Users
item.description: Every AI agent tutorial follows the same script: Import LangChain Define some tools Call the LLM in a loop Ship it! And it works. In the demo. In the notebook. In the conference talk. Then you...

item.title: Meet Complexity
item.description: In the last post, we talked about complexity in broad strokes — the idea that some of it is necessary, and some of it isn’t, and that software design is largely about eliminating what isn’t and...

item.title: Dez tecnologias essenciais em .NET
item.description: Este material está alinhado à conversa em vídeo “10 coisas que você deve fazer na sua aplicação .NET”, publicada no YouTube pelo canal Eximiaco — Excelência Tecnológica. Gabriel Kohlrausch conduz...

item.title: I Audited 5 Popular awesome-nodejs Packages for Their Environment Variable Documentation. Here's the Scorecard.
item.description: awesome-nodejs is the canonical curated list of quality Node.js packages. Quality implies documentation. I wanted to see how these packages handle .env documentation specifically — the section...

item.title: Meet Software Design
item.description: In plain terms: A plan for building software so that it’s easy to understand and change. You know it when you see it. You may not be able to explain why, but deep down there’s a nagging...

item.title: Understanding Seq2Seq Neural Networks – Part 7: Generating the Output with Softmax
item.description: In the previous article, we were transforming the outputs to the fully connected layer. A fully connected layer is just another name for a basic neural network. This fully connected layer has two...

item.title: 9 Developer Utilities You Can Add to Claude Desktop in 30 Seconds
item.description: You're in the middle of debugging. You need a UUID. Or you need to decode a JWT. Or you need to hash something. You alt-tab to a browser, find an online tool, paste your data into someone else's...

item.title: All Data and AI Weekly #234 - 23 March 2026
item.description: All Data and AI Weekly #234 - 23 March 2026 ( AI, Data, Agentic AI, Cortex Code, NiFi, Iceberg, Polaris, Streamlit, Python, Java, SQL, MCP, LLM, RAG, Cortex AI, AISQL, Search, Unstructured Data )...

item.title: How I Built and Deployed a Free Email Validation API with Python and FastAPI
item.description: I recently built and deployed my first API — an email validation tool built with Python and FastAPI. Here's how I did it and what I learned along the way. Why Email Validation? Every app that...

item.title: Publisher pulls horror novel ‘Shy Girl’ over AI concerns
item.description: Hachette Book Group said it will not be publishing “Shy Girl” over concerns that artificial intelligence was used to generate the text.

item.title: Delve accused of misleading customers with ‘fake compliance’
item.description: An anonymous Substack post accuses compliance startup Delve of “falsely” convincing “hundreds of customers they were compliant” with privacy and security regulations.

item.title: The AirPods Pro 3 are $50 off right now, nearly matching their best-ever price
item.description: Less than a week ago, Apple announced the forthcoming AirPods Max 2, a pair of over-ear headphones that leverage the company’s H2 chip for AI-powered live translation, conversation awareness, and...

item.title: Why Wall Street wasn’t won over by Nvidia’s big conference
item.description: Despite investor fears of an AI bubble, Nvidia's latest conference shows that most in the industry aren't concerned by that possibility.

item.title: Here are 20 of our favorite outdoor deals from REI’s Member Days Sale
item.description: REI’s latest sale is in full swing. The outdoor retailer’s exclusive shopping event runs through Monday, March 23rd, bringing with it a batch of discounts on Garmin watches, tents, portable...

item.title: The gen AI Kool-Aid tastes like eugenics
item.description: Like many people, director Valerie Veatch was intrigued when OpenAI first released its Sora text-to-video generative AI model to the public in 2024. Though she didn't fully understand the...

item.title: FBI Warns Russian Hackers Target Signal, WhatsApp in Mass Phishing Attacks
item.description: Threat actors affiliated with Russian Intelligence Services are conducting phishing campaigns to compromise commercial messaging applications (CMAs) like WhatsApp and Signal to seize control of...

item.title: Oeuf is a punishing platformer in a cozy shell
item.description: The funny shape of eggs is the curious lifeblood of Oeuf, the new physics platformer by prolific developer Increpare Games. In a gaming landscape saturated with complex systems dropped into simple...

item.title: Dreame’s self-cleaning L10s Pro Ultra is nearly $1,000 off its original list price
item.description: Amazon’s Big Spring Sale doesn’t kick off until next week, but the retailer is already dropping a host of good deals, especially on robot vacuums. We recently saw Ecovacs’ Deebot X8 and X9 Pro...

item.title: The new MacBook Pro is still fast as hell
item.description: Apple's flagship 16-inch MacBook Pro has reigned supreme in the world of creator-focused laptops since its M-series processor overhaul in 2021. Since then, we've mostly seen the same design with...

item.title: An early contender for movie of the year

item.title: Gemini task automation is slow, clunky, and super impressive
item.description: I've been testing out Gemini's new task automation on the Pixel 10 Pro and the Galaxy S26 Ultra, which for the first time lets Gemini take the wheel and use apps for you. It's limited to a small...

item.title: I Tried DoorDash’s Tasks App and Saw the Bleak Future of AI Gig Work
item.description: I recorded videos of myself doing laundry, scrambling eggs, and walking around the park in DoorDash’s new Tasks app, where gig workers are paid to train AI.

item.title: Kodiak CEO says making trucks drive themselves is only half the battle
item.description: This year is shaping up to be a big one for self-driving trucks. In addition to Aurora's plan to deploy hundreds of autonomous big rigs and Waabi expanding into robotaxis, you've also got Kodiak...

item.title: Cyberattack on a Car Breathalyzer Firm Leaves Drivers Stuck
item.description: Plus: The FBI admits it’s buying phone data to track Americans, Iranian hackers disrupt medical care at Maryland hospitals, and more.

item.title: Oracle Patches Critical CVE-2026-21992 Enabling Unauthenticated RCE in Identity Manager
item.description: Oracle has released security updates to address a critical security flaw impacting Identity Manager and Web Services Manager that could be exploited to achieve remote code execution. The...

item.title: 'Jury Duty Presents: Company Retreat' Almost Makes Corporate Culture Seem Fun
item.description: The Amazon Prime prank series amplifies the hijinks of workplace dynamics, while showing how people find purpose—and community—in their jobs despite impossible situations.

item.title: Trivy Supply Chain Attack Triggers Self-Spreading CanisterWorm Across 47 npm Packages
item.description: The threat actors behind the supply chain attack targeting the popular Trivy scanner are suspected to be conducting follow-on attacks that have led to the compromise of a large number of npm...

item.title: CISA Flags Apple, Craft CMS, Laravel Bugs in KEV, Orders Patching by April 3, 2026
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Friday added five security flaws impacting Apple, Craft CMS, and Laravel Livewire to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV)...

item.title: The improved battery-powered Starlink Mini is here
item.description: My Starlink Mini has radically changed the way I live. It's the biggest reason I can work remotely from my converted van, providing fast and reliable internet in places that traditional 4G and 5G...
